name: Raleigh (United States)

colors.symbolism: the hoist half; from the state flag of North Carolina
the fly half; from the state flag of North Carolina
the central oak tree, evoking Raleigh's nickname 'the City of Oaks'

description: A field divided vertically — red at the hoist, white at the fly — with a green oak tree centred across the dividing line.
history: Used by the City of Raleigh, the capital of North Carolina, founded in 1792 and named for Sir Walter Raleigh, the English colonial-era patron of the failed Roanoke Colony.

features.symbolism: Red and white, the principal colours of the flag of North Carolina.
Raleigh's nickname, 'the City of Oaks', from the many oaks lining its streets.
